author: Shibdat Singh
title: Chashma-e-Hisab, Hissa Awwal
gloss: Arithmetic for the students of Lower Primary Classes, Part I.
notes: Primary arithmetic textbook, Part I, 2800 copies. '(Designed for educational purposes.—concluded)'.

author: Shibdat Singh
title: Chashma-e-Hisab, Part II
notes: Dash-continuation of s5; Part II of the arithmetic text. Closes URDU—SCIENCE (MATHEMATICAL).

title: Nagma Sukhan Kamal
gloss: A collection of love verses in Urdu.
publisher: Bhai Jot Singh, Sant Singh, Book-sellers
notes: Urdu love-verse printed in GURMUKHI (Punjabi) script — the script-crossing phenomenon in miniature: Urdu-language content set in the Sikh liturgical script for Punjabi-reading (often Sikh) audiences; a Sikh bookseller (Bhai Jot Singh). Directly on-theme for the script/language disaggregation the project studies. Section is 'URDU (PUNJABI CHARACTER)'.

author: Brown, Miss E. M., M.D.
title: Dai Gari ke Asul
notes: Urdu printed in ROMAN script — a midwifery handbook by an American/European woman doctor (Miss E. M. Brown, M.D.), from the Ludhiana Mission Press; the missionary-medical-women's-health nexus AND the Roman-Urdu experiment (the colonial/missionary push to romanize Urdu). Both a female-authored medical text (cf. p31 s12) and a script-politics artefact. Composite pagination 8,116,4.

author: Nur Bakhsh, Maulvi, M.A.
title: Specimen page of the Holy Quran with a new English translation and commentary
gloss: Specimen page of the Holy Quran with a new English translation and commentary. 1 sheet.
publisher: Maula Bakhsh, The New Lyall Press
notes: A single specimen sheet (prospectus) for a new English Quran translation-with-commentary by an M.A. maulvi, 5000 copies — the modernist project of an English Quran (Muslim self-translation for an anglophone audience, anticipating the great 20th-c. English tafsirs); opens the BILINGUAL sections. The Arabic-English pairing captures the imperial-language encounter directly.

author: Muhammad Mehdi
title: Khisal-ush-Shia
gloss: A pamphlet describing what a true Shia ought to be from the religious point of view. Translated by Muhsin Ali, Lahore.
notes: 'Qualities of the Shia' (Arabic text with Persian rendering) — Shia devotional-ethical instruction; the Arabic-Persian bilingual format serving the learned Shia readership. Continues the strong Shia presence of the quarter. Dual date 1327 Hijri.

title: Namaz
gloss: Muhammadan daily prayers with Sindhi translation.
notes: Muslim daily prayers (Arabic) with Sindhi translation — devotional bilingual for the Sindhi Muslim reader; again via Pokhar Das of Shikarpur (the Sindhi-market channel, cf. p22) and, strikingly, the Arya (Hindu-reform) Steam Press printing a Muslim prayer-book — the press as neutral commercial infrastructure across communal content. Reg 142.
